original_filename: Bartos et al. (2026) Effect of Artificial Intelligence on Learning.pdf

decisions.reason: Bartos et al. (2026) - meta-meta-analysis of AI on learning. High-priority for next session.

Re-triggered from DEFER. Highest-tier evidence on AI-and-learning effects: bias-adjusted SMD = 0.196 vs. unadjusted 0.629; extreme heterogeneity; no subgroup shows consistent benefit. UPDATE proposals to performance-paradox, novice-vulnerability, metacognitive-laziness, cognitive-debt, leveling-effect.
Workbench renamed from bartos-et-al-2026-effect-of-artificial-intelligence-on-learning to bartos-ai-learning-meta-meta-2026 for citability and to match KB slug convention.

AUTO-mode high-confidence distillation. 1 source distillation + 5 UPDATE proposals (performance-paradox, novice-vulnerability, metacognitive-laziness, cognitive-debt, leveling-effect). No NEW concepts (umbrella reviews calibrate existing literatures rather than naming new constructs). Stage 4 self-critique skipped per high-confidence rubric.
Abbreviated critique per high-confidence rubric. AI failure checklist clean: no hallucinated citations, no statistical drift, no false novelty (no NEW concept proposed).

validate_drafts.py exit 0 on second pass. First pass surfaced 5 broken_wikilinks (resolved by renaming workbench dir to canonical slug bartos-ai-learning-meta-meta-2026) and 1 missing_related (cognitive-offloading link added to source distillation Supports section).

Source distillation moved to sources/bartos-ai-learning-meta-meta-2026.md. 5 UPDATE proposals applied to performance-paradox, novice-vulnerability, metacognitive-laziness, cognitive-debt, leveling-effect (each: 1 paragraph in body + 1 Related link + 1 frontmatter source line + 1 Sources section line). Auxiliary scripts run (sync-source-links, build-index, update_readme_counts: sources 60→61, total 124→125).
